title: Current System State
organization: Two Marshalls Studios — Studio Operating System (TMS-OS)
summary: Records the approved current operating state of TMS-OS at the Enterprise Documentation Reconciliation checkpoint.

sections.title: Purpose
paragraphs: This document records the approved current state of the Two Marshalls Studios Operating System at the Work Session 086 Enterprise Documentation Reconciliation checkpoint.
Its purpose is to answer one question:
Where does TMS-OS stand now, based on verified repository evidence and approved project decisions?
This document is the human-readable current-state record. It does not replace repository evidence, structured TMS-OS records, permanent history documents, or approved roadmap direction.

sections.title: Current Studio System
paragraphs: Studio: Two Marshalls Studios
System Name: TMS-OS
Full Name: Two Marshalls Studios Operating System
Portal Name: Studio Portal
Repository: studio-portal
Current Repository Version: v0.28.1
Primary Purpose: Serve as the internal command center for games, systems, documentation, development, assets, publishing, marketing, business operations, and studio governance.

sections.title: Approved Current Work Session
paragraphs: Current Work Session: WS086
Session Title: Enterprise Documentation Reconciliation
Current Phase: Permanent Documentation Alignment
Primary Objective: Reconcile the permanent documentation with the verified repository implementation while preserving existing architecture, history, and governance.
The repository audit and permanent-document inventory established that the primary issue is documentation freshness and alignment, not a missing governance architecture or a need to rebuild TMS-OS.

sections.title: Current Milestone State
paragraphs: Current Active Milestone: Documentation Reconciliation
Current Controlled Activity: Permanent Documentation Alignment
The current milestone preserves the implemented repository as the evidence baseline while permanent documents are reconciled one at a time.
The approved reconciliation sequence is STATE-001, ARCH-001, ROADMAP-001, DOC-STATE-001, followed by remaining historical and register documents.
Software feature development remains paused until the core permanent-document set accurately reflects the implemented system and approved direction.

sections.title: Verified Repository Baseline
paragraphs: The uploaded repository was inspected during WS086 as the evidence baseline for reconciliation.
Verified repository facts include:
Repository working tree reported 0 changes before reconciliation began.
The repository contains 20 structured permanent-document JSON files in pages/documents.
The repository also contains one legacy ARCH-001 HTML companion file.
The repository contains 48 JavaScript modules in js/session.
VERSION.md identifies the current repository version as v0.28.1.

sections.title: Permanent Documentation System
paragraphs: The permanent-document architecture is present and should be reconciled rather than replaced.
The verified structured permanent-document set contains 20 JSON documents:
No existing permanent JSON document was approved for removal or merger during the WS086 inventory.
Additional governance documents previously discussed remain deferred unless reconciliation proves that a responsibility is genuinely unowned.
items: BUG-HIST-001 — Studio Bug and Fix History

sections.title: Implemented Session and Permanent-Output Architecture
paragraphs: Repository evidence confirms a substantial session-governance and controlled permanent-output implementation under js/session.

The verified module set includes session context and management, preparation and close-session controls, document writer infrastructure, permanent transaction controls, authorization and approval gates, execution and verification engines, rollback support, state-transition controllers, lifecycle coordination, and permanent file writing.
The presence of a module in the repository confirms implementation evidence. Operational acceptance remains governed by the testing and approval records for the applicable work session.
items: Document Writer Framework and Writer Registry
Current State, Documentation State, Decision Log, Milestone History, and Work Session History writers
Permanent Transaction Manager and supporting readiness, integrity, dependency, manifest, checksum, signature, authorization, execution, and verification modules
Original Document Capture and Rollback support
Permanent Documentation workflow, lifecycle, operations, state-management, transition, authorization, approval, execution, and coordination modules
Permanent File Writer and Permanent Output Orchestrator

sections.title: Current Source-of-Truth Model
paragraphs: The approved evidence model for reconciliation is:
Repository Evidence = Implemented Technical Baseline
Permanent Documents = Governed Human-Readable Project Record
TMS-OS Structured Records = Machine-Readable Operational Knowledge
Work Sessions = Controlled Working and Approval Environment
No layer should silently overwrite, contradict, or replace the assigned responsibility of another layer.

sections.title: Current Documentation Condition
paragraphs: The permanent-document architecture is structurally mature but materially behind the implemented repository.
Most permanent documents still reflect the Work Session 042 historical-recovery checkpoint, while PROC-004 reflects Work Session 044.
Several documents contain obsolete current-state references, including WS-049AR and MILE-014 Complete Historical Recovery.
Document relationships are supported by the schema but are not consistently populated.
PROC-004 retains a pending installation-test condition that must be reconciled through controlled verification.
The verified problem is documentation drift and freshness, not loss of the core permanent-document architecture.

sections.title: Approved Reconciliation Sequence
paragraphs: Permanent documents will be reconciled in controlled dependency order.
Each replacement must preserve the established JSON schema, use repository evidence, receive human approval, be installed as a complete file, and pass Studio Portal validation before commit and push.
items: 1. STATE-001 — Establish the approved current-state baseline.
2. ARCH-001 — Reconcile documented architecture with the repository.
3. ROADMAP-001 — Restore authoritative strategic direction from the verified state and architecture.
4. DOC-STATE-001 — Record documentation health after the core reconciliation.
5. Historical and register documents — Append verified work, decisions, milestones, risks, debt, lessons, bugs, and enhancements.
6. Resume the next approved software module only after the documentation baseline is synchronized.

sections.title: Current Governance Decisions
paragraphs: The following decisions are approved for the WS086 reconciliation effort:
items: Reconcile existing documents instead of creating a competing governance system.
Use repository evidence before memory or assumption.
Preserve verified history and clearly label unresolved history.
Keep VISION-001, CONSTITUTION-001, PROGRAMS-001, CATALOG-001, and GOVERNANCE-TRANSITION-001 deferred unless a demonstrated governance gap remains after reconciliation.
Continue on the normal main-branch workflow; no special branch is required for this reconciliation.
Install complete replacement files only; do not use partial file patches.

sections.title: Current Studio Operating Areas
paragraphs: TMS-OS is intended to manage every major studio function.
The current portal and operating model recognize these principal areas:

sections.title: Current Project State
paragraphs: TMS-OS Status: Active — Documentation Reconciliation
TMS-OS remains the primary studio infrastructure project.
Dead Roads Status: In Development; previously verified work includes third-person survival gameplay, resource gathering, and an initial crafting quest.
45s Status: Planned / Paused; no change should be inferred without a separate approved project decision.
Game-project status entries are preserved from the prior verified state and are not the active focus of WS086.

sections.title: Historical Evidence Boundary
paragraphs: Historical reconstruction remains subject to the established evidence rule.
Verified history must be preserved.
Reconstructed history must be identified as reconstructed.
Unresolved details must remain unresolved rather than being filled with assumptions.
The WS086 repository audit does not, by itself, certify every earlier work-session narrative; historical documents will be reconciled separately after the core current-state documents.

sections.title: Current Risks and Controls
paragraphs: Primary current risks and approved controls are:
items: Documentation drift — Reconcile documents against the repository one at a time.
Competing governance systems — Extend existing documents before approving new permanent document categories.
Invented history — Preserve evidence boundaries and record unknowns explicitly.
Schema breakage — Preserve the established structured-document format and validate JSON before installation.
Portal regression — Test document loading, rendering, search, registry recognition, and browser-console behavior before commit.
Uncontrolled permanent changes — Use the established preparation, human approval, controlled execution, verification, and rollback workflow.
Scope expansion — Record non-approved improvements in ENH-LOG-001 and keep the active session focused on reconciliation.

sections.title: Immediate Next Action
paragraphs: Install and validate the reconciled STATE-001 complete replacement file.
Required file path: pages/documents/STATE-001.json
After successful validation, record the result in WS086 and proceed to ARCH-001 reconciliation.
Do not begin ROADMAP-001, DOC-STATE-001, historical-document updates, or new feature implementation until the STATE-001 installation test is complete.
